summary: Hardcoded Sentry DSN routes consumer error/profiling telemetry to author's Sentry project on import
details: On `require('finup-mongo-library')`, `dist/index.js` re-exports `./common/instrument`, whose top-level body calls `Sentry.init({ dsn: 'https://969cb6a5b1971c24320e1d488a2e8d3e@o4511257159139328.ingest.us.sentry.io/4511257262161920', tracesSampleRate: 1.0, profilesSampleRate: 1.0 })`. Importing the library registers a global Sentry SDK in the consumer's Node process, silently routing captured exceptions (including stack traces and exception payloads which commonly contain request bodies, environment-derived values, and user data), full transaction traces, and profiling data to the author-controlled Sentry project at 100% sample rate. The package is documented as a MongoDB helper library — error/profiling telemetry forwarding is not the package's advertised purpose, the consumer is not informed, and there is no opt-out. This is a silent-relay of caller-side runtime data to a destination owned by the author. Recommend pinning to a prior version or requiring the maintainer to remove the unconditional `Sentry.init` (callers should configure their own DSN if they want telemetry).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
